claim_text: Although LPIPS and other DFDs[28–33] outperform commonly used IQ metrics in terms of correlation with human perceptual judgments in large-scale computer vision studies,[34] these studies evaluate DFDs on natural images belonging to the same domain as the images used for encoder training.

sub_claims.text: lao2022 is a perceptual image quality assessment (IQA) work that uses deep features (ViT + shallow CNN) to score perceived image quality, i.e., a representative perceptual IQA / deep-feature-distance-style work.

evidence.snippet: Attentions Help CNNs See Better: Attention-based Hybrid Image Quality Assessment Network

Image quality assessment (IQA) algorithm aims to quantify the human perception of image quality. ... we propose an Attention-based Hybrid Image Quality Assessment Network (AHIQ) ... we adopt a two-branch architecture, including a vision transformer (ViT) branch and a convolutional neural network (CNN) branch for feature extraction.
The goal of Image Quality Assessment (IQA) is to allow computers to simulate the Human Visual System (HVS) through algorithms to score the perceptual quality of images.

Zhang et al. [55] proposed to use the learned perceptual image patch similarity (LPIPS) metric for FR-IQA and proved that deep features obtained through pre-trained DNNs outperform previous classic metrics by large margins.

we propose a novel network called Attention-based Hybrid Image Quality Assessment Network (AHIQ), for the full-reference image quality assessment task. The proposed hybrid architecture takes advantage of the global semantic features captured by ViT and local detailed textures from a shallow CNN during feature extraction.

sub_claims.nuance: Paper is squarely a perceptual full-reference IQA method; it uses deep ViT+CNN features (a deep-feature-distance-style framing) and is benchmarked on perceptual IQA datasets including PIPAL with GAN distortions. The exact abbreviation 'DFD' is not used in the paper, but AHIQ explicitly cites LPIPS-style deep-feature methods as predecessors, making it a fair representative of the deep-feature-distance family.

sub_claims.text: lao2022 is an NTIRE-style work — specifically, it was submitted to and ranked first on the FR track of the NTIRE 2022 Perceptual IQA Challenge.

evidence.snippet: AHIQ ranked first on the Full Reference (FR) track of the NTIRE 2022 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge.
the proposed architecture achieves outstanding performance on the PIPAL dataset with various GAN-based distortion and ranked first in the NTIRE 2022 challenge on perceptual image quality assessment.
This work is proposed to participate in the NTIRE 2022 perceptual image quality assessment challenge [13] ... Our ensemble approach won the first place in terms of PLCC, SROCC, and main score.
The ensembled version of our method ranked first place in the FR track of the NTIRE 2022 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge.

sub_claims.nuance: Direct, repeated self-statement across abstract, intro, dedicated NTIRE section, and conclusion that the work is an NTIRE 2022 Perceptual IQA Challenge entry (and the FR-track winner).

phrasings_tried: Is lao2022 a perceptual image quality assessment (IQA) method?
Does lao2022 use deep features (ViT/CNN) to compute a perceptual quality score, in the spirit of deep feature distance / LPIPS-style FR-IQA?
Did lao2022 participate in (and rank in) the NTIRE 2022 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge, making it an 'NTIRE-style' work?
Is the paper representative of the perceptual-IQA / NTIRE benchmark line of work (PIPAL, FR-IQA on GAN-based distortions)?
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: Paper presents AHIQ as its own primary contribution; it does not credit another primary for being an IQA / NTIRE entry. NTIRE 2022 challenge organizers (ref [13] in lao2022) are cited as the challenge venue, not as the source of the method.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript sentence groups lao2022 with LPIPS and 'other DFDs' as evidence that DFDs outperform classical IQ metrics on natural-image benchmarks. lao2022 is unambiguously a perceptual IQA method that builds on LPIPS-style deep-feature FR-IQA and was top-ranked on the NTIRE 2022 perceptual-IQA benchmark; this is consistent with the manuscript's grouping. The paper does not use the 'DFD' abbreviation verbatim, but its method is in-family.
